function.name: web_scrape
function.description: Fetch ONE web page and return its main content as clean markdown (boilerplate stripped) plus metadata. Get the url from web_search first. ALWAYS pass a 'query' describing what you're looking for — it returns only the relevant sections; WITHOUT a query you get just the start of the page and may miss info deeper down. Does not run JavaScript.

url.description: The http(s) URL to fetch (e.g. a web_search result's url).

query.description: What you're looking for on the page. Strongly recommended: returns only the relevant sections (keeps output small). Without it you get the page from the start.

max_chars.description: Max characters of content to return (default 6000; the system trims further to fit the context window).

offset.description: Character offset to resume from when paging through a long page (default 0).
